item.content:encoded: It's technically +99, but still! A great read.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: That all seems like good stuff, I approve! Of course I do not speak for my co-author but I think he'd probably be fine with it too.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Got two proposals that can work for this article. The first, we have a mention of the GOC, which can be linked to their hub page, thus facilitating a wikiwalk in that direction. Additionally, the mention of MTF-ψ-7 "Home Improvement" can be linked to their MTF page entry, facilitating a wikiwalk to other articles they are mentioned in. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

The property has been surrounded by a warehouse complex under the front company "Security Construction Programs" which is designated Containment Sector-77. Due to the stability of SCP-2281, once per year MTF-ψ-7 "Home Improvement" stages a high-level training on this property, and personnel within Containment Sector-77 should consult their Level 3 supervisor as to their instructions during that time. SCP-2281 currently has no research projects associated with it.

To this:

The property has been surrounded by a warehouse complex under the front company "Security Construction Programs" which is designated Containment Sector-77. Due to the stability of SCP-2281, once per year MTF-ψ-7 "Home Improvement" stages a high-level training on this property, and personnel within Containment Sector-77 should consult their Level 3 supervisor as to their instructions during that time. SCP-2281 currently has no research projects associated with it.

Changing this:

Interviewed: Calvin Denton [GOC Agent]

To this:

Interviewed: Calvin Denton [GOC Agent]

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wonder what would happen if this thing went on the same road as SCP-973?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this for being heartwarming (an emotional response one rarely gets from skips) in a relatively subtle, understated manner.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this. Fairly run-of-the-mill anomaly used to worldbuild in an interesting way. +1'd.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Neutral no-vote. Not all that interesting and the interview log at the end really didn't have the hook I was hoping for.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm imagining a deeper-voiced Navi from the Zelda games just suddenly popping up.

"Hey! Listen! Slow down!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …especially since the voice comes from a blind spot directly behind the driver.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked this one. Upvoted. The idea of a highway spirit trying to be 'helpful' is kinda cute.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Depends on how helpful a disembodied voice yelling to slow down is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Great skip; upvoted. But I do want to know for narrative purposes - after the local deity became a highway boss, did the accident rate on that stretch of road decrease or increase?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I also have mixed feelings over the part about "sparing the skip"… 2281 is the first anomaly Denton had encountered, and I would imagine that his GOC handlers would like to know how he was introduced to the world of anomalies.

Also, it's a hell of a coincidence that the Foundation has found and captured Denton in a random operation.

While the article is good, I will stay neutral for the time being. I do like how 2281 tries to enforce traffic in his capacity as a "road spirit" of sorts.

EDIT: Amend to downvote. The coincidence reminds me of a Hong Kong drama-type of scenario.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I mean, I have to formally give Cimmerian all relevant credit to avoid ulcers, but…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Means a lot Esko. I know it took a lot to admit that I had part in writing a good SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I went ahead and feexed those errors (the colons were definitely my fault anyway). I imagine Roget won't mind, but if he does my apologies. ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
On 06/14/2009, Foundation agents discovered that Calvin Denton had not been killed in 1999. This discovery was made after Mr. Denton entered Foundation custody following participation as a Calvin Denton in an operation opposing Foundation interests.

Is…is Calvin Denton his title, too? Or an occupation? "I hired a Calvin Denton to unclog my sink last week." Also, why aren't there colons after the name of the agent interviewing him?

Other than technical nitpicks, a good SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You're welcome!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this, Thank You!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Still upvoted, but i would suggest getting rid of the whole "agent was there so he found it" dealie.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Was great fun to work on this. Very happy with how it turned out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Special thanks to my collaborator, Cimmerian, and to my good friends Dmatix and TwistedGears for giving me shit for this as I was writing it so that I had to make it as good as it could be. Shoutout to Randomini for his feedback as well.
